seoTitle: Willoughby East vs Willow Tree — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Willoughby East and Willow Tree. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Willoughby East vs Willow Tree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Willoughby East and Willow Tree.
heading: Willoughby East vs Willow Tree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Willoughby East, New South Wales and Willow Tree, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Willoughby East has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Willow Tree (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Willoughby East or Willow Tree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Willoughby East has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Willow Tree scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Willoughby East scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Willow Tree scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Willoughby East or Willow Tree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Willoughby East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
